text:
There was a girl named Lucy who lived in a world where everything moved backwards—from birdsong to daylights-saving time. Her school was enchanted, a place where days spun backward and history was rewritten nightly. One peculiar afternoon, Lucy found herself lost in history class, staring at an old desk. An owl hooted slowly, reversing her lecture.

"Who wrote this?" Lucy asked the owl.

"It's me," it replied. "Your great-great-grandmother."

"Were my ancestors nerdy like me?"

The owl chuckled.

"Why wouldn't they be?"

Lucy smirked.

"Yeah. The only interesting book I read was 'Pride and Prejudice.'"

The owl fluttered away.

"You should read 'Moby-Dick.' It’s a classic."

Lucy laughed. "I might just."

The classroom rewound, and they sat under a branch where autumn leaves spun.

That week, Lucy found stories from centuries past were oddly relevant.

Later, the owl returned,
